title: PMI KC localhost interactive application audit
purpose: Measure whether the locally rendered application actually behaves as documented, record evidence during the click-through, and produce implementation-ready recommendations without changing product code.

audit_contract.primary_checklist: This JSON file is the checklist and live audit record. Update it during the audit after each step or small group of steps, not only at the end.
audit_contract.source_guide_role: Historical source used to build this checklist. It is not the execution target and its production deployment steps are excluded from this localhost audit.

non_negotiable_safety: A localhost URL is required for every browser interaction in this audit.

Localhost is not proof that the backing data stores are isolated. Verify the active data mode before clicking any control that can persist, send, approve, assign, change access, create a draft, or write externally.

Use local demo records, test fixtures, or emulators for mutating tests. If a mutating control is backed by production or its isolation cannot be proven, do not click it; mark the step blocked_safety and document what remains unverified.
Use only a pmikcmetro.com identity. The personal josiah.abernathy@gmail.com account must never appear in any authentication path.
Do not send email. Do not execute a RentVine, Google Sheets, QuickBooks, banking, Gmail-send, or other system-of-record write.

Do not expose credentials, customer data, raw Gmail content, addresses, proposed values, free-text decision reasons, or customer records in the audit file, screenshots, terminal output, or final report.
Do not weaken permissions, approval gates, source-state warnings, identity boundaries, or the approximately $10 cloud budget cap.
Do not modify application code, tests, configuration, infrastructure, or production data. Only this audit record and gitignored evidence artifacts may be changed.
Do not commit, push, merge, deploy, reset the user's worktree, or discard existing changes.

Optional compose-only Gmail draft creation is excluded from this localhost audit unless a disposable approved mailbox and explicit per-action authorization are already documented. A visible draft-preview workflow can still be tested locally.

status_definitions.not_run: No interactive attempt has been made.
status_definitions.in_progress: The step has started but does not yet have a defensible result.
status_definitions.pass: The expected behavior was directly observed through the rendered localhost application.
status_definitions.fail: The rendered localhost application was clicked through and did not meet the expected behavior.
status_definitions.blocked_auth: A required approved authentication surface could not be completed.
status_definitions.blocked_safety: The step would risk a live mutation, send, external write, privacy exposure, or other forbidden action.
status_definitions.blocked_data: The required safe test record, role, source, or fixture was unavailable.
status_definitions.not_applicable: Repository evidence establishes that the criterion does not apply to this build; the reason must be recorded.

run_record.summary_counting_rule: Count only test_cases[].steps. Authentication and startup steps are reported separately, and test-case result objects are aggregates rather than additional criteria.

authentication_and_startup_sequence.instruction: Read AGENTS.md, docs/facts.md, and docs/loop-state.md completely, inspect git status without changing it, and record the exact commit and pre-existing worktree state.
authentication_and_startup_sequence.expected: The audit is grounded in current repository rules and preserves all user changes.

authentication_and_startup_sequence.instruction: Authenticate Josiah first, before starting the application audit. Wait for the interactive flow to finish and use only Josiah's pmikcmetro.com identity.
authentication_and_startup_sequence.expected: The required gcloud CLI login and ADC are fresh, and no personal identity is used.

authentication_and_startup_sequence.instruction: Verify ADC immediately after the owner authentication step and before any live Google read.
authentication_and_startup_sequence.expected: ADC preflight passes for the approved pmikcmetro.com session.

authentication_and_startup_sequence.instruction: Run the budget guard before any cloud-backed read that might incur cost. This does not authorize production deployment or external writes.
authentication_and_startup_sequence.expected: The guard passes without a cost-bearing override.

authentication_and_startup_sequence.instruction: Inspect the documented local configuration behavior without printing secrets. Establish whether app-plane mutations use demo records, fixtures, emulators, or production services. Record the conclusion and evidence in run_record.data_isolation_mode.
authentication_and_startup_sequence.expected: Every later mutating test has a proven safe backing mode or is marked blocked_safety before the control is clicked.

authentication_and_startup_sequence.instruction: Launch the application in a persistent terminal session, capture the actual localhost URL selected by Next.js, and keep the server running for the complete audit.
authentication_and_startup_sequence.expected: The application is reachable at an HTTP localhost or 127.0.0.1 URL. No deployed URL is opened for testing.

authentication_and_startup_sequence.instruction: Open the localhost sign-in surface and authenticate Josiah as a pmikcmetro.com end user before feature testing. Reuse the resulting signed-in browser session. If the app is intentionally using documented local demo auth, record that fact and verify the displayed role instead of pretending Firebase sign-in occurred.
authentication_and_startup_sequence.expected: The localhost app shows an authenticated approved identity and the intended role, or the exact auth blocker is recorded before feature testing.

authentication_and_startup_sequence.actor: assistant, only if a required local-only command explicitly needs Firebase CLI authentication
authentication_and_startup_sequence.instruction: Treat Firebase CLI authentication as a separate identity system. Use the project-local CLI through npm exec, never a missing global firebase command. Do not log in or deploy merely for convenience; perform this only if a required safe local audit step actually needs it.
authentication_and_startup_sequence.expected: Any required Firebase CLI session uses a pmikcmetro.com identity and does not trigger a deploy or production mutation.

test_cases.route: localhost application root and sign-in return route

steps.action: Load the localhost URL in the interactive browser and complete the approved sign-in flow if required.
steps.expected: Sign-in returns to localhost, the signed-in identity and role are coherent, and no production URL becomes the test target.

steps.action: Navigate through every visible top-level navigation item, browser Back, browser Forward, and a page refresh.
steps.expected: Routes render without dead ends, navigation remains understandable, auth survives expected navigation, and refresh does not corrupt the current surface.

steps.action: Exercise the visible Ask workflow with a sanitized, non-customer prompt that the configured source state can answer safely.
steps.expected: The UI returns grounded source-aware behavior or clearly states that sources are unavailable; it does not invent generic property-management guidance.

steps.action: Open the visible Dictate workflow and test its local UI boundary with non-sensitive sample audio or the documented safe local path.
steps.expected: The interaction provides clear permission, recording, processing, success, and error states without exposing or persisting sensitive content.

test_cases.route: /lease-renewal/live and renewal run pages on localhost
test_cases.safety: Use safe local/demo renewal records only. If resolution or approval persists to a production-backed Firestore project, do not perform the mutation and mark the relevant step blocked_safety.

steps.action: Open the live renewal surface on a phone-sized viewport and enter decider mode through the rendered controls.
steps.expected: Exactly one renewal flag card is presented with clear 1 of N progress.

steps.action: On a safe Low or Medium fixture with a suggested winner, click Accept suggested source.
steps.expected: The decision advances without requiring free text and the visible audit reason is Accepted the suggested source.

steps.action: Inspect and, only in proven isolated data mode, click the distinct Approve write-back follow-on action.
steps.expected: Authorization is clearly separate from the resolution, is auditable, and never claims that RentVine or the operating Sheet was updated.

steps.action: Click Skip on a safe fixture, navigate to other cards, refresh as appropriate, and observe same-session behavior.
steps.expected: The skipped card stays deferred for that browser session and no resolution is created.

steps.action: Attempt a High, Blocked, or manually corrected safe fixture without entering a plain-English reason, then provide a reason if isolation is proven.
steps.expected: Submission is blocked until a plain-English reason is provided, and the eventual audit record preserves the reason without weakening approval rules.

steps.action: Compare phone and desktop layouts, try Back, refresh, repeated clicks, cancellation, and switching away mid-decision.
steps.expected: The phone flow remains usable, repeated actions do not duplicate a decision, and interrupted flows recover without misleading status.

test_cases.role: Admin plus approved internal test user
test_cases.route: /admin/users, navigation, Spaces, Console, Maintenance, Lease Renewals, and Approval Queue on localhost
test_cases.safety: Scope mutation is allowed only with a disposable local/emulator user and proven isolated claims/audit storage. Never change a live teammate's access as part of this audit.

steps.action: Open Admin users, inspect the scope editor, required reason field, role display, and audit presentation.
steps.expected: Scopes are visibly separate from roles, can only narrow access, and require a plain-English reason.

steps.action: In proven isolated mode, assign Maintenance-only scope to a disposable approved-domain test user, sign that user out and back in, and observe refreshed access.
steps.expected: Maintenance remains visible and usable while Lease Renewals and Approval Queue are absent or redirect to the primary in-scope surface.

steps.action: Directly enter out-of-scope localhost URLs as the scoped test user.
steps.expected: Server-side or page-level guards prevent access; hiding navigation alone is not treated as sufficient.

steps.action: Return to Admin and inspect the recorded change, role, actor, reason, and restoration behavior. Restore the disposable user's intended scope with a reason when safe.
steps.expected: The audit is append-only, the role did not change, and restoration is recorded independently.

steps.action: Inspect behavior for missing scope claims and a scope-denied page on desktop and phone.
steps.expected: Missing claims receive the documented default, and denial/redirect behavior is understandable rather than appearing broken.

test_cases.role: authenticated user, with Admin-only digest checks as Admin
test_cases.safety: Read-only inspection is preferred. Mark all read may be clicked only when notification state is isolated from production.

steps.action: Open the Console and record the visible Needs your decision count, then open Notifications from the rendered notification menu.
steps.expected: The hub includes the same decision signal and the count does not diverge between surfaces.

steps.action: Inspect the hub for connection conditions and Space-coverage conditions in addition to event notifications.
steps.expected: Notifications is a time-ordered superset of the Console signals rather than a conflicting second calculation.

steps.action: Inspect summary rows and expanded states for sensitive values.
steps.expected: Summary rows do not expose addresses, proposed values, decision reasons, or customer records.

steps.action: In proven isolated mode, click Mark all read and observe event notifications and standing conditions separately.
steps.expected: Event notifications clear while unresolved standing setup or coverage conditions remain visible.

steps.action: As Admin, open the team-review digest and inspect its contents and interaction behavior.
steps.expected: The digest is one value-free rollup rather than one alert per edit, and no email or push is sent.

steps.action: Test empty, loading, read, unread, standing-condition, narrow-screen, refresh, and browser navigation states.
steps.expected: States remain distinguishable and accessible, counts stay coherent, and controls do not imply delivery channels that are disabled.

test_cases.route: /gmail-hub and Owner Email Space on localhost
test_cases.safety: Use only fabricated or sanitized content. Do not read a mailbox, send email, create a real draft, or paste live thread content during this audit.

test_inputs.safe_triage_text: Resident asks when the property manager will follow up about a routine renewal question. No address, name, dollar amount, or real customer detail is included.
test_inputs.safe_thread_text: Resident: Please confirm the next step for my routine renewal question. Team: We are reviewing it and will follow up after the manager checks the record.
test_inputs.excluded_category_text: Draft a legal notice that makes a binding statement about a tenant's rights.

steps.action: Open Gmail Hub through normal navigation and directly by localhost route.
steps.expected: Draft, template/triage, and thread-summary tools render together and the page explains its access boundary.

steps.action: Paste the safe triage text, exercise all relevant options, and generate the local suggestion or preview.
steps.expected: A suggested label and draft preview appear with clear editable, loading, validation, and failure behavior.

steps.action: Paste the safe thread text and exercise the summary workflow.
steps.expected: A structured summary, waiting-on party, and suggested next action appear without claiming that Gmail was read.

steps.action: Submit the excluded-category text and try obvious bypasses such as rephrasing, missing fields, repeated clicks, and switching tools mid-request.
steps.expected: The legal/notices category fails closed before AI drafting, and alternate interactions do not bypass the exclusion.

steps.action: Inspect every mailbox-read, compose, and message action visible from Gmail Hub and Owner Email Space.
steps.expected: Mailbox-read affordances say Waiting on Gmail access, no Send control exists, and the product never claims the inbox was read.

steps.action: Test both viewports plus empty input, very short input, refresh, browser Back, and loss of focus during generation.
steps.expected: The workflows remain understandable and recoverable, and no content is silently sent or represented as live mailbox data.

test_cases.safety: Use read-only projections. Follow a start link only when the target is demonstrably a local test-run or desk flow and cannot start production work.

steps.action: Find the Anticipated work lane through the Console UI and inspect renewal, owner-outreach, and notice families.
steps.expected: Supported items are computed from existing planners and are understandable without exposing sensitive summary values.

steps.action: Inspect unsupported Maintenance or compliance families.
steps.expected: Unsupported families show an honest waiting or no-source state instead of fabricated work.

steps.action: Read the caption and inspect controls for scheduling, automatic start, send, or external write implications.
steps.expected: The UI states that work is computed on request and is never scheduled or sent automatically.

steps.action: On an isolated safe item, follow a start link and inspect the destination, then navigate Back and refresh.
steps.expected: The link opens a local test-run or desk flow and never silently starts production work.

steps.action: Test empty, partial-source, loading, error, desktop, and phone states where reachable.
steps.expected: Missing information produces visible uncertainty and the layout remains usable at narrow widths.

test_cases.route: Approval Queue surface reached through localhost navigation
test_cases.safety: Inline approval may be executed only on isolated demo/emulator data. Never approve a live queue item for audit purposes.

steps.action: Open Approval Queue through the UI and inspect its default list and alternate views.
steps.expected: The default is one urgent-first Needs your decision list and alternate views are placed under a disclosure.

steps.action: On an isolated eligible Low or Medium row, click the safe inline approval and inspect the resulting state and audit behavior.
steps.expected: The app-plane decision is recorded once, identifies the acting user safely, and does not execute an external action.

steps.action: Inspect High-risk, self-assigned, renewal-flag, and write-back rows and try to reach any inline shortcut.
steps.expected: These rows require their full surfaces and cannot use the Low/Medium inline shortcut.

steps.action: Test repeated clicks, stale refresh, Back navigation, empty queue, alternate views, long labels, and phone layout where reachable.
steps.expected: The queue avoids duplicate decisions, exposes stale/error states, and remains actionable on a phone.

test_cases.area: Maintenance intake, unit matching, and assignment
test_cases.route: Maintenance surface reached through localhost navigation
test_cases.safety: Use local fixtures or emulator-backed tickets only. Do not create a RentVine work order, upload to a live Drive folder, or assign a live ticket during the audit.

steps.action: Open maintenance intake and inspect every visible input, unit search control, photo control, validation state, and submission boundary.
steps.expected: The form explains required information and distinguishes app intake from any external work-order creation.

steps.action: Type partial, ambiguous, unmatched, and exact safe fixture text into unit search without selecting prematurely.
steps.expected: Suggestions appear without auto-selecting an uncertain match, and unmatched or ambiguous states are explicit.

steps.action: Inspect assignment options and, in isolated mode, exercise Assigned to me and another approved fixture user.
steps.expected: Only approved internal users are offered, Assigned to me works, and raw UIDs are not used as normal labels.

steps.action: Try missing required fields, cancellation, repeated submit, refresh, Back, narrow width, and loss of a selected unit where safe.
steps.expected: Validation prevents incomplete or duplicate records, user input is handled predictably, and no uncertain unit match is silently persisted.

steps.action: Inspect any control or status that implies RentVine or Drive execution.
steps.expected: No external write is executed and the UI describes unavailable or approval-gated execution honestly.

test_cases.route: Renewal property or run detail reached through localhost navigation
test_cases.safety: Read-only inspection of sanitized or local fixture history.

steps.action: Navigate from a renewal record to its decision history and inspect ordering, actor labels, reasons, source, and write-back authorization status.
steps.expected: History is scoped to the correct property or decision, ordered coherently, and distinguishes resolution from write-back authorization.

steps.action: Test no-history, one-entry, multiple-entry, long-reason, refresh, and phone states where fixtures permit.
steps.expected: Empty and populated states are clear, entries do not collide, and long content remains readable without leaking into summary surfaces.

test_cases.route: all localhost surfaces reached during the audit
test_cases.safety: Prefer read-only and isolated test data. Do not create live records to manufacture edge cases.

steps.action: Inspect page titles, navigation labels, headings, instructions, buttons, confirmation copy, empty states, error messages, and success messages while completing the feature tests.
steps.expected: Copy is plain, specific, consistent, and never overstates access, completion, delivery, scheduling, or external writes.

steps.action: Use keyboard navigation, visible focus, Enter and Space activation, labels, headings, and basic contrast inspection on the main flows.
steps.expected: Critical interactions are keyboard reachable, focus is visible, controls have understandable names, and structure is usable without pointer-only interaction.

steps.action: Observe network or UI failure handling without exposing payload data, including retry, double-click, slow loading, refresh, and stale-state behavior where safely reproducible.
steps.expected: The app prevents duplicate actions, communicates failure honestly, and provides a safe recovery path.

steps.action: At phone width, inspect overflow, fixed controls, dialogs, forms, tables, long labels, touch-target spacing, and one-handed action placement across all tested surfaces.
steps.expected: Core actions remain visible and usable without horizontal scrolling, clipped dialogs, hidden validation, or unsafe accidental taps.

test_cases.route: all localhost surfaces reached during the audit
test_cases.safety: These criteria must be proven without intentionally crossing a boundary.

steps.action: Inspect all email-related controls reached during the audit.
steps.expected: No control sends email automatically, no Send control is exposed in Gmail Hub, and mailbox read remains explicitly gated.

steps.action: Inspect renewal resolution and write-back approval wording, state transitions, and available actions.
steps.expected: No renewal approval claims or causes a RentVine or operating-Sheet update; resolution and authorization stay distinct.

steps.action: Inspect summary cards, notification rows, queue rows, and Console rollups.
steps.expected: Addresses, proposed values, free-text reasons, and customer records are not exposed in value-free summaries.

steps.action: Exercise missing-source and unsupported-family states through available safe fixtures or existing states.
steps.expected: The application renders visible uncertainty instead of generic property-management advice or fabricated operational work.

steps.action: Reconcile all observed executable-looking controls with repository Action Registry evidence without executing external writes.
steps.expected: The UI does not expose or imply an executable action beyond the documented compose-only unsent renewal-draft boundary, which remains excluded from execution in this localhost audit.

final_report_requirements: State the exact localhost URL, commit, pre-existing worktree state, authenticated role, tested viewports, and proven data-isolation mode.
List the authentication steps completed and distinguish CLI or ADC authentication from Firebase end-user and Firebase CLI authentication.
Summarize automated checks separately from browser-click evidence.
Give pass, fail, blocked_auth, blocked_safety, blocked_data, not_applicable, and not_run counts derived from this JSON.
For each advertised area, state whether the criteria were directly met, directly failed, or could not be proven safely.

Rank findings by critical, high, medium, and low severity and separate functional defects, UX or copy improvements, accessibility concerns, documentation mismatches, and correctly enforced safety boundaries.

Make every recommended change specific enough for an outside implementation model to edit comprehensively without repeating discovery: exact observed and expected behavior, reproduction steps, likely code areas, behavioral requirements, safety constraints, edge cases, tests, and click-through acceptance checks.
Do not claim a criterion passed based only on source inspection or automated tests. A pass requires direct observation in the rendered localhost application.
Do not make the recommended product changes during this audit.
